text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Fuentes-Delgado, Marisol
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Oncology/Interventional Radiology]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology / Interventional Radiology

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy, Right Hepatic Lobe, Segment VI
  Specimen Type: CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy
  Procedure: CT-guided core needle biopsy, 18-gauge coaxial needle, right hepatic lobe, segment VI lesion
  Clinical Indication: New 1.4 x 1.2 cm hypoattenuating hepatic lesion, segment VI, on surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis; prior history of triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, left breast, stage IIB; treated with neoadjuvant pembro, dose-dense Adriamycin/cyclo, pacli, followed by left modified radical mastectomy with contralateral prophylactic mastectomy; adjuvant Xeloda and pembro; now with new hepatic lesions suspicious for metastatic recurrence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number and designated "CT-guided core needle biopsy, right hepatic lobe, segment VI." The specimen consists of three tan-white to pale gray cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments ranging from 0.8 cm to 1.4 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are firm and have a slightly irregular texture. No grossly identifiable fat or discrete nodules appreciated. The specimen is entirely submitted in one cassette (A1). Representative frozen section imprint cytology was performed at time of biopsy to confirm adequacy (touch prep positive for malignant epithelioid cells). All material submitted for histologic processing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Microscopic examination of all three core biopsy fragments reveals sections of hepatic parenchyma with near-complete replacement by an infiltrating malignant epithelial neoplasm. The background liver parenchyma, where present, shows mild periportal fibrosis and mild steatosis, without significant hepatitis activity or cirrhosis. There is no bile duct proliferation or perisinusoidal fibrosis to suggest primary hepatic etiology.

Tumor Architecture and Cytology:
The neoplasm is composed of highly pleomorphic epithelioid cells arranged in irregularly anastomosing nests, solid sheets, and interconnecting cords separated by a desmoplastic fibrous stroma. Areas of central geographic necrosis are prominent, occupying approximately 30-35% of the tumor volume on the submitted sections. The tumor cells demonstrate markedly enlarged, hyperchromatic nuclei with coarse chromatin and one to multiple conspicuous nucleoli. The nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio is high. Cytoplasm is scant to moderate in amount, pale eosinophilic, and focally vacuolated. Cell borders are poorly defined. Mitotic figures are readily identified, averaging 18 per 10 high-power fields, including atypical mitoses. No squamous differentiation, glandular (tubular) differentiation, mucin production, or spindle cell component is identified. No intranuclear inclusions or bile production is seen, arguing against a primary hepatocellular carcinoma.

Stroma and Vascular Invasion:
The intervening stroma is densely fibrotic with scattered chronic inflammatory infiltrate including lymphocytes and plasma cells. Lymphovascular invasion is identified focally within the fragment (one focus of tumor cell cluster within a thin-walled vascular space). No perineural invasion is definitively identified on the limited biopsy material.

Adjacent Liver:
The non-neoplastic hepatic parenchyma at the biopsy edge demonstrates mild macrovesicular steatosis (estimated 10-15% of hepatocytes), mild lobular disarray, and mildly expanded portal tracts with fibrotic change, consistent with the patient's known history of type 2 diabetes and metabolic dysfunction. No viral cytopathic effect, bile plugs, or iron deposition is noted on routine staining.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Immunohistochemistry Panel (performed on Specimen A, block A1):

All immunostains were performed using validated antibodies on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue with appropriate positive and negative controls.

Breast Lineage Markers:
  GATA3: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear staining in >80% of tumor cells) -- supports mammary/urothelial origin; combined with clinical history, strongly supports breast primary.
  GCDFP-15 (BRST-2): FOCALLY POSITIVE (weak-to-moderate cytoplasmic positivity in approximately 20% of tumor cells) -- supports breast primary.
  Mammaglobin: NEGATIVE -- note: absence does not exclude breast origin, particularly in triple-negative/basal-like subtype.

Epithelial/Cytokeratin Markers:
  Pan-Cytokeratin (AE1/AE3): POSITIVE (strong, diffuse) -- confirms carcinoma.
  CK7: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse) -- consistent with breast, lung, or upper GI primary.
  CK20: NEGATIVE -- effectively excludes colorectal and Merkel cell primary.
  CK5/6: POSITIVE (moderate, patchy) -- consistent with prior basal-like phenotype of known primary.
  CK14: POSITIVE (focal) -- further supports basal-like phenotype.

Organ-Specific Exclusionary Markers:
  TTF-1 (thyroid transcription factor-1): NEGATIVE -- excludes lung adenocarcinoma and thyroid primary.
  CDX2: NEGATIVE -- excludes colorectal and intestinal differentiation.
  Napsin A: NEGATIVE -- excludes lung adenocarcinoma.
  PAX8: NEGATIVE -- excludes renal cell carcinoma, Mullerian tumors, and thyroid primary.
  HepPar-1: NEGATIVE -- excludes hepatocellular carcinoma.
  Arginase-1: NEGATIVE -- excludes hepatocellular carcinoma.
  Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE -- excludes neuroendocrine differentiation.
  Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE -- excludes neuroendocrine differentiation.

Hormone Receptors / HER2 (to confirm triple-negative phenotype at metastatic site):
  Estrogen Receptor (ER, SP1 clone): NEGATIVE (0% nuclear staining; Allred score 0/8) -- confirms hormone receptor-negative status maintained at metastatic site.
  Progesterone Receptor (PR, 1E2 clone): NEGATIVE (0% nuclear staining; Allred score 0/8) -- confirms PR-negative status maintained.
  HER2 (4B5 clone, IHC): 1+ (faint, incomplete membrane staining; <10% of invasive tumor cells) -- NEGATIVE per ASCO/CAP guidelines.
    NOTE: HER2 FISH REFLEXED per laboratory protocol given 1+ IHC result and metastatic clinical context -- RESULT PENDING AT TIME OF REPORT ISSUANCE. Addendum to follow.

Proliferation Marker:
  Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): POSITIVE in approximately 78% of tumor cell nuclei (markedly elevated, consistent with high-grade, rapidly proliferating tumor; decreased from 92% in original diagnostic biopsy but notably higher than 55% seen in surgical resection specimen post-neoadjuvant therapy, suggesting re-expansion of a highly proliferative clone).

Additional Markers:
  p53 (DO-7 clone): STRONG DIFFUSE NUCLEAR POSITIVITY (>90% of tumor cells) -- aberrant p53 expression pattern (consistent with TP53 gain-of-function hotspot mutation, e.g., p.R248W, as identified on prior molecular profiling).
  EGFR (31G7 clone): POSITIVE (2+, membranous) -- consistent with prior primary tumor staining.
  E-cadherin (36B5 clone): POSITIVE (intact membranous staining) -- consistent with ductal (non-lobular) carcinoma histology.
  AR (androgen receptor, AR441 clone): NEGATIVE -- consistent with prior negative AR status.
  PD-L1 (22C3 assay, Dako): CPS = 12 (POSITIVE, though notably decreased from CPS 28 at initial diagnosis; immune contexture may have been altered by prior Keytruda therapy and radiation. PD-L1 expression remains above the 10 CPS threshold.)

Special Stains:
  Mucicarmine: NEGATIVE -- no intracellular or extracellular mucin production.
  Periodic acid-Schiff with diastase (PAS-D): Glycogen present in background hepatocytes; tumor cells are PAS-D negative.
  Reticulin: Disrupted reticulin framework in tumor areas; normal reticulin pattern in adjacent hepatic parenchyma -- pattern consistent with metastatic carcinoma rather than primary hepatocellular carcinoma.

ADDENDUM PENDING:
  HER2 FISH (fluorescence in situ hybridization): Result pending. Expected turnaround: 3-5 business days. Addendum will be issued upon completion.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A -- CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy, Right Hepatic Lobe, Segment VI:

METASTATIC CARCINOMA, consistent with breast primary.

  -- Morphology: High-grade invasive carcinoma with solid sheet and nested architecture, central necrosis, marked nuclear pleomorphism, and brisk mitotic activity; no hepatocellular, neuroendocrine, glandular, or squamous differentiation.
  -- Immunophenotype: Consistent with triple-negative breast carcinoma (ER-/PR-/HER2 1+ by IHC; HER2 FISH pending). GATA3 positive, GCDFP-15 focally positive, CK7 positive, CK5/6 positive (basal-like phenotype). Negative for TTF-1, CDX2, napsin A, HepPar-1, arginase-1, PAX8, chromogranin, synaptophysin, CK20 -- effectively excluding lung, colorectal, primary hepatocellular, renal, Mullerian, and neuroendocrine primaries.
  -- Clinical correlation: Findings are consistent with recurrence of the patient's known triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, left breast, BRCA1-mutated, basal-like subtype.
  -- PD-L1 CPS: 12 (positive; 22C3 assay).
  -- Ki-67: 78%.
  -- HER2 FISH: PENDING (addendum to follow).
  -- Lymphovascular invasion: Present (focal, within biopsy cores).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
------------------------------------------------------------------------

The histologic and immunohistochemical findings in this CT-guided hepatic core needle biopsy are diagnostic of metastatic carcinoma. The immunophenotype (GATA3+, GCDFP-15 focal+, CK7+, CK5/6+, CK14+; ER-, PR-, HER2 1+ by IHC, TTF-1-, CDX2-, CK20-, HepPar-1-, arginase-1-) in the appropriate clinical context of a known BRCA1-mutated triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast strongly supports this hepatic lesion as a metastatic deposit from the patient's known breast primary. The triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) immunophenotype is maintained at the metastatic site, consistent with preservation of the original tumor's hormone receptor and HER2 status. PD-L1 CPS of 12 is noted; while lower than the 28 CPS recorded at initial diagnosis, this may reflect clonal evolution, prior immunotherapy exposure (pembro), and/or tumor heterogeneity; the result remains above the 10-point threshold. Ki-67 proliferative index of 78% reflects re-expansion of a highly proliferative clone, intermediate between the post-neoadjuvant surgical specimen (55%) and the original diagnostic biopsy (92%). Molecular re-profiling of this metastatic specimen is recommended to assess for clonal evolution, acquired resistance mechanisms, and eligibility for targeted therapies. HER2 FISH result is pending and will be reported in a subsequent addendum.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Surgical Pathologist]
SIGN-OUT STATUS: Preliminary (pending HER2 FISH addendum)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
